title: Expressing opposition to Central Business District Tolling Program of New York City.
summary:
OVERVIEW: This bill expresses Congress’s disapproval of a plan by New York City to charge drivers a fee to enter parts of Manhattan. It aims to halt the implementation of this congestion pricing program.

WHO IS AFFECTED:
- Drivers who travel into parts of Manhattan.
- Residents and businesses in/around the tolled area. 

WHAT CHANGES:
- Could block New York City’s congestion pricing plan.
- May require federal funds be redirected away from the project.

WHAT'S DEBATED:
- Supporters say: The program unfairly burdens commuters and hinders access to Manhattan.
- Critics say: It interferes with local decisions and a revenue source for mass transit. 

STATUS: This bill has been introduced in the House of Representatives and is currently awaiting consideration by relevant committees.
status: Referred to the House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure.

title: Reserved for the Minority Leader.
summary:
OVERVIEW: This bill would allow the House Minority Leader to designate a staff member to participate in certain intelligence briefings, similar to what is already allowed for the Speaker of the House and Minority Whip. It aims to ensure the Minority Leader receives timely access to important national security information.

WHO IS AFFECTED:
- The Office of the House Minority Leader.
- Intelligence community briefers/staff. 

WHAT CHANGES:
- Another staff member can attend briefings.
- Expands existing access for leadership beyond the Whip.

WHAT'S DEBATED:
- Supporters say: This ensures the Minority Leader is fully informed on critical national security matters.
- Critics say: It could potentially broaden the circle of individuals with access to sensitive information without clear need. 

STATUS: The bill has been introduced in the House and referred to the Intelligence Committee.

title: Reserved for the Minority Leader.
summary:
OVERVIEW: This bill changes how the House of Representatives can designate a member as “Minority Leader.” Currently, it’s an informal process; this would make it official through a vote.

WHO IS AFFECTED:
- Members of the House of Representatives.
- Political parties within the House.

WHAT CHANGES:
- The Minority Leader will be chosen by a majority vote. 
- It establishes a formal, recorded vote for this position.
- The bill details specific procedures for nominations and voting.

WHAT'S DEBATED:
- Supporters say: This ensures stability and transparency in leadership selection.
- Critics say: It could politicize the process and diminish internal party autonomy. 

STATUS: The bill has been introduced in the House and is currently awaiting consideration by a committee.
